title: drive_leg_result
description: One transport leg's driver dispatch outcome -- REPORTED, not self-graded. The judge room independently grades the captured evidence.

evidence_refs.description: Paths or retained ids for the captured evidence (frames/screenshots/traces).

frames_dir.description: Directory of captured frames/screenshots for this leg, if any.

blockers.description: Honest blockers if evidence could not be captured (e.g. a JSON-degraded visual surface).

post_drive_evidence_ref.description: vscode legs only: path or retained id for a SECOND vscode bridge capture (visual.open kind=vscode + visual.observe/snapshot) taken AFTER driving the live session to its target state -- not the preflight capture. Required before a vscode leg can be scored anything other than degraded-evidence; leave empty and report the blocker honestly if the post-drive capture could not be taken.

post_drive_session_handle.description: vscode legs only: the live session_handle the post-drive capture was taken against (e.g. "s2"). Should match the session handle the scenario was actually driven to, proving the capture reflects the driven-forward state rather than the original preflight session.

harness_used.description: replay | live | (empty if no primary_story session.new call was needed). The harness this leg's PRIMARY session.new call actually used -- report honestly even when it differs from what args.live_profile implied. The story's record_leg_result.star deterministically forces the verdict to degraded-evidence, with an explicit policy-violation cause, if this is "live" while args.live_profile was empty: an explicit profile= is the ONLY live authorization, and a replay-miss (a session.new(harness="replay") call that hits a host.agent.* call with no matching cassette episode) is a hard MCP error to report as the blocker, never a signal to open a new session live on your own initiative.

profile_used.description: The harness profile actually passed to this leg's primary session.new call when harness_used is "live" (should equal args.live_profile -- report honestly if it does not).

summary.description: What the driver actually attempted for this leg.
